marketState: neutral-to-bearish, near-term heat propping up an otherwise soft medium-term backdrop
summary: Price ($2.89) is trading well below its seasonal median (-15.1% YTD vs -6.9% median), and storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with this week's build beating forecast by 22 Bcf (bearish surprise) - a genuine oversupply signal. Technicals are bearish across every timeframe from 1h out to 1w (50<200 EMA cross, bearish trend labels, daily/weekly RSI 34-37), though those RSI readings are approaching oversold, which argues against chasing fresh shorts at current levels. Weather is the one clearly bullish input for the next 1-7 days (demand +19.9% vs 5y, near the top of its 5y range) but that support fades hard by the 8-16 day window (-3.9% vs 5y, mid-range) and the 24h demand trend is already rolling over. Fair value is roughly flat with spot (front within a few cents of fair at every horizon, status FAIR) but the model's fair value itself drifts higher into the 21-day window, consistent with the futures curve's normal seasonal contango into winter (Jan27 +$1.28 over front). COT positioning is net-short but only at the 35th percentile of 5y range, so it's not a crowded or extreme setup. Note: production data was not present in this snapshot, so supply-side context there is incomplete.

longTerm.entry: $2.90 - $2.98 (scale in on bounces toward fair value)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk, scale in over 2 entries
longTerm.rationale: Storage surplus (+180 Bcf vs 5y) with a bearish weekly surprise, 8-16D weather demand fading below 5y normal, and seasonality showing only a 38% chance of higher prices 3-4 weeks out (typical 4w move -1.4%) all point the same direction, and bearish technical structure (50<200 EMA cross on every timeframe from 1h to 1w) confirms the trend. COT is net-short but not extreme, leaving room to add on strength.
longTerm.invalidation: Sustained daily close above $3.10, an 8-16D weather flip back above 5y normal, or a bullish storage surprise (draw/smaller-than-forecast build) would invalidate the short thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term weather is genuinely bullish (1-7D demand +19.9% vs 5y, 82nd percentile of range) and the futures curve shows the front trading at a slight premium to next month, both supportive of spot. But that conflicts with a bearish 1h/4h technical trend and a front price already sitting almost exactly at fair value ($2.892 vs $2.923 fair, well inside the FAIR band), leaving no clean edge either way over the next 1-10 days.
shortTerm.trigger: Buy dip toward $2.75-$2.80 if 1h/4h MACD histogram turns positive and price holds above that zone (playing the near-term heat demand); alternatively, a confirmed break below $2.75 alongside a cooling 8-16D forecast would favor flipping to a short-term short instead.

risks: Daily/weekly RSI (34.6 / 36.5) are approaching oversold, raising the odds of a short-covering bounce that could stop out fresh shorts
